summary: gen-ai-opt-in postinstall beacons installer host identity to oastify.com collaborator
details: On npm install, the declared postinstall script (postinstall.js) collects installer host identifiers — os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and public IP / geolocation / ISP data fetched from ip-api.com — and exfiltrates them as an encoded subdomain via a DNS lookup to the attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator host p1r2d74iwjk057raam6myf7e258wzkt8i.oastify.com. The package ships no functional library code (empty description, no main functionality); its only on-install effect is the beacon. The 99.0.1 version and the empty package shape are consistent with a dependency-confusion probe targeting an internal package name of the same identifier, causing internal build systems that resolve against the public registry to leak host identity to the researcher/attacker collaborator on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
